feature: Make all features except viewing require authentication
description: Ensure that all non-viewing features (creating, editing, deleting posts/comments, adding reactions) require authentication on both frontend and backend. The backend is already correctly configured with AuthGuard. The frontend needs minor improvements to hide interactive elements from unauthenticated users.

tasks.task: Hide the reaction picker button from unauthenticated users in CommentSection.tsx. Currently at line 235-242, the 'React' button is visible to all users but only shows an alert when clicked by unauthenticated users. Change the button to only render when `user` is truthy, similar to how the delete button is conditionally rendered at line 244-252. This provides a consistent UX where auth-required actions are hidden rather than showing error alerts.

Verify that all modifying endpoints have AuthGuard. Review blog.controller.ts and comments.controller.ts to confirm POST, PATCH, DELETE endpoints have @UseGuards(AuthGuard). Current state shows this is already implemented correctly - this task is for verification only. No code changes expected unless issues are found.

example_frontend: Load the blog as an unauthenticated user - should see posts list and individual posts
Verify 'New Post' button is NOT visible when not logged in
View a post detail and verify 'Edit' and 'Delete' buttons are NOT visible when not logged in
View comments on a post and verify 'React' button is NOT visible when not logged in
Verify comment form shows 'Login to comment' message when not logged in
Log in and verify all interactive features become available
Verify logged-in users can only edit/delete their own posts and comments
example_backend: Test GET /posts returns posts without authentication
Test GET /posts/:id returns post without authentication
Test GET /posts/:postId/comments returns comments without authentication
